content:
How the death of the traditional search engine results page forces a complete rewrite of the creator business model.

11 min read
2 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Sascha Bosshard on UnsplashI woke up on a Tuesday morning , made my coffee, and opened Google Search Console like I had done every morning for the past eight years.
My traffic was down 37% from the previous month.
Not a slow decline. Not a seasonal dip. A cliff.
I refreshed the page. Same numbers. I checked Analytics. Same story. I ran a few test searches for my best-performing articles — the ones that had been ranking #1 for years — and that's when I saw it.
Google had answered my reader's question. Without sending them to me.
An AI-generated summary sat at the top of the search results. It had pulled the key insights from my article, synthesized them into a neat little paragraph, and presented them to the user. No click required. No visit to my site. No ad revenue. No email capture. No relationship.
The search engine that had built my business had just become my biggest competitor.
